statements.impact_statement: imaplib is never imported (verified across src/). The bridge is a Newznab/SABnzbd HTTP service with no IMAP client, so the injectable command path is unreachable.
poplib is never imported (verified across src/). The service speaks no POP3, so the injectable command path is unreachable.

configparser is never imported (verified across src/). Configuration comes from environment variables and is never written to .ini files, so no attacker-controlled value is serialized through configparser.
tarfile is never imported (verified across src/). The app extracts no archives, so tarfile.extractall and its symlink-escape path are never reached.
tarfile is never imported (verified across src/). No archive parsing occurs, so the streaming-mode EOF denial-of-service is unreachable.

This is a Windows-specific privilege escalation via the PCbuild VPATH landmark. The image is a Linux build; /usr/local is root-owned and immutable at runtime while the process runs as unprivileged nobody (65534), so no adversary can plant the landmark or an alternative Lib directory.

The flaw is in the stdlib http.client proxy-tunnel/host header path. All HTTP egress uses httpx (httpcore), not http.client, and no proxy is configured. The only http.client use is the Dockerfile healthcheck against a fixed localhost URL with no proxy and no adversary-controlled host or headers.
asyncio.ProactorEventLoop is Windows-only and the advisory states non-Windows platforms are not affected. The image is Linux (SelectorEventLoop); the app also does not use asyncio at all.
webbrowser is never imported (verified across src/). A headless server never calls webbrowser.open, so the shell-injection path is unreachable.

The use-after-free requires re-using an lzma/bz2/gzip decompressor instance after a MemoryError. None of lzma, bz2 or gzip is imported (verified across src/); httpx decodes gzip/deflate responses with per-response one-shot zlib decoders, which the advisory explicitly lists as not affected.

The flaw is in BusyBox wget (CRLF injection in the HTTP request-target). The container CMD execs the Python interpreter directly (exec form, no shell); the app spawns no subprocess and never invokes wget or any BusyBox applet. The healthcheck uses Python's urllib, not wget.
